summary: Iter-35 Email Template Editor + Partner deep-link testing. Backend (13/13 pytest) passes end-to-end: list/get/update/reset/preview + header+footer wrapping + variable interpolation + app_url auto-fill + forgot-password uses DB template + seed idempotency. Frontend admin editor (E-Mail-Vorlagen tab) renders 10 templates grouped by category, WYSIWYG + HTML toggle + save + iframe preview work. CRITICAL deep-link bug found: backend generates /partner/dashboard URL but the actual frontend route is /partner-dashboard — deep-links from partner emails DO NOT open the user modal.

ui_bugs.issue: navigator.clipboard.writeText() is called without catching the Promise rejection. In browsers without clipboard permission (headless, http, restricted contexts) a NotAllowedError crashes the app with an uncaught runtime error overlay that blocks interaction (e.g. Reset button).

integration_issues.flow: Partner deep-link from email -> open user modal
integration_issues.issue: CRITICAL: Backend helpers.py _partner_deep_link() builds `{FRONTEND_URL}/partner/dashboard?openUser=<id>` but the frontend route defined in App.js is `/partner-dashboard` (hyphen). Visiting /partner/dashboard?openUser=<id> redirects to /partner-dashboard and the openUser query param is LOST, so the user-detail modal NEVER auto-opens from a real email link. Verified: visiting /partner-dashboard?openUser=<valid_id> directly DOES open the modal and strip the param, proving the frontend handler itself works — only the emitted URL is wrong.

action_items: FIX (CRITICAL): change _partner_deep_link() in /app/backend/helpers.py to use '/partner-dashboard?openUser=<id>' (hyphen, matching App.js route) — or add a route alias '/partner/dashboard' in frontend App.js that renders PartnerDashboard. Without this, the deep-link feature does not work in production partner emails.

FIX (MEDIUM): wrap navigator.clipboard.writeText(token) in try/catch (or .catch()) in /app/frontend/src/components/admin/EmailTemplateEditor.js line 183 so a clipboard permission denial does not crash the React tree / block the reset button.

NICE-TO-HAVE: expose a backend /api/partner/users/{user_id}/exists-in-my-dashboard lookup OR make the existing submission check tolerant of invalid ObjectIds so the frontend 'User nicht in Ihrem Dashboard gefunden' toast fires deterministically for malformed IDs (current behavior: URL is cleaned but toast visibility was not 100% reproducible in the automated run).
critical_code_review_comments: helpers.py _partner_deep_link hard-codes '/partner/dashboard' which does not match the frontend route '/partner-dashboard'. Single source-of-truth for route names (constants or env var PARTNER_DASHBOARD_PATH) would have prevented this drift.

EmailTemplateEditor.insertVariable: unhandled promise from navigator.clipboard.writeText() causes a red dev-overlay that blocks subsequent interactions (repro: click any variable chip in headless/insecure contexts, then try to click Reset).

server.py admin_update_email_template silently filters to (subject, body_html, description) — good, but returning 400 on empty allowed-keys payload while accepting partial updates is fine. Consider validating body_html size/length to prevent 1MB+ payloads.

seed on startup wraps in a broad try/except and only logs a warning — idempotency behavior is correct (insert vs _setOnInsert-like merge), but a failure here leaves the app with missing templates and no health signal.

success_rate.frontend: 80% — editor flows work; deep-link broken due to URL mismatch

seed_data_creation: none — used existing seeded data. Test-edited templates are reset by pytest teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest file /app/backend/tests/test_email_templates_iter35.py is stable and reusable for regression. After main agent fixes the /partner/dashboard vs /partner-dashboard route mismatch, re-run only the frontend deep-link test: login partner -> goto /partner-dashboard?openUser=69e778e88cd17cafc506b995 and confirm [role=dialog] appears. Also retest the clipboard try/catch fix by clicking any [data-testid^=email-template-var-] chip and verifying no uncaught-error overlay appears.
rca of the issue: Deep-link break: backend/helpers.py line 184 returns f'{base}/partner/dashboard?openUser={user_id}' but frontend/src/App.js line 54 only defines <Route path='/partner-dashboard' ...>. React Router does not match '/partner/dashboard', so the session redirect sends the partner back to '/partner-dashboard' (base route) losing the ?openUser query string. The PartnerDashboard useEffect that reads the param never fires with the intended id. Reproduction: open https://guided-journey-5.preview.emergentagent.com/partner/dashboard?openUser=69e778e88cd17cafc506b995 as a logged-in partner -> lands on /partner-dashboard with no openUser, no modal. Mitigation: either (a) update _partner_deep_link to use '/partner-dashboard?openUser=...' OR (b) add a second Route path='/partner/dashboard' pointing at the same PartnerDashboard component. Option (a) is safer (single route).
